reviews.review_description: I just downloaded this app today and I like the whole set up, but I don’t actually know how to play piano at all. I was wondering if there was some sort of guide to learn?
Although,  I cannot  play piano, thank-you for this realistic option. Beautiful.
If your actually trying to play songs then, oh wait the hand tracking is so bad. Well atleast there’s different instruments exept it’s just piano that makes the sounds of other instruments.
The keys are very light and precise. Other pianos are also included. The only drawback is that I think the "MR" ending should be removed from the piano.
It’s obviously not gonna be perfect but its hard to even mess around with it, I like air drums better
I didn't like it one key at a time

this piano sucks, tried to play and guess what? only could play with 1 finger.. it’s okay for a free game thats why i gave it 3 stars. but don’t get this game for full pieces. sucks. you can get pianovision or buy a real piano and it will be better
I got so excited when I saw the video for this!! But when I got in, I could barely even play! As a real pianist, I was very disappointed.
Best game ever so immersive and fun
I tried this it wouldn’t work with my hands I could only hit the keys one at a time with the controller.

developer_response.body:
Hello PowerUpQueen, you should use Hand Tracking to play the Piano. If not activated ->
1. Activating Hand Tracking on the Meta Quest (User-Side)
Access the Settings:
Put on your headset and open the Settings menu.
Enable Hand Tracking:
Navigate to Hands and Controllers.
Enable the option Enable Hand Tracking.
Set Up Automatic Switching (Optional):
In the Automatic Switching section, you can configure whether the Quest should automatically switch between controllers and hand tracking when you put down the controllers.
Function Test:
Place your controllers aside and hold your hands in front of the cameras. You should see that hand tracking is now activated.

reviews.review_title: Do u have to have a piano?
reviews.review_description: Do u have to have a piano?

Nice app to get away and relax with some casual playing on a piano. It would be nice to be able to slide one finger across multiple keys to trigger multiple keys in one sliding motion since each key now has to be individually pressed. Love the passthrough mode for piano since it’s the only way to fit a piano in my place. New to VR and found virtual piano rendering was very impressive. Piano is great for casual playing for fun and relaxing.

reviews.review_title: The best Piano app I've purchased
reviews.review_description:
I really enjoy your room options and don't mind paying for each one (they are beautiful). It really creates a relaxing & realistic vibe. 

May I make a suggestions? Can you give us the option to position the piano in different directions so that we can get a full view of each room?  For instance, in one room, the piano is facing more towards the wall, but I would prefer to have the option to turn it towards the living room area and so forth in every room.

reviews.review_title: the best game ever, here’s a shrek

reviews.review_description:
So this piano game is ok ish I had ZERO clue how to use it and it’s VERY hard to play one key at once 

I play piano in reality and it’s SOOOOOOOO much easier 

Although the game does posses nice graphics and a gentle feel but please it needs a BIG upgrade

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for your feedback. PIANO is of course not aimed at professional piano players, although we would of course also like to inspire you. With a real piano you can of course feel the keys and it is therefore a little easier to orient yourself by hand. We hope that PIANO can inspire some people to play the piano and have fun with it.
We continue working on PIANO to give you best results. To achieve the best possible hand tracking, please ensure that the room is well lit.

reviews.review_description:
Summary: A good try at something that would be a dream of mine to be able to do... FREAKING PLAY PIANO ON VR!!! I'm an actual pianist in real life, so I may be a little too harsh on this game.

Pros:
-You can press keys and they play notes
-Hand tracking (Always cool regardless)
-Cool settings and pianos

Cons:
-Hand tracking just doesn't allow you to play with fingerings. Your best bet is to use your pointer finger and hit individual notes. I can't play anything professional on here... tbh, I can't even play chopsticks on here without it glitching or it having my fingers play the wrong notes, or press a note without playing it.

Solution: It was nice to play around with for a while, but let's be honest... technology isn't there yet... YET

GAME ON MY FRIENDS! :-]

developer_response.body: thanks for trying Piano. Of course, this app is not intended for professional pianists.
